rationale: Step 0's gate test fails: no objective verifier exists for headline quality and 'compelling' is a taste judgment, not a machine-decidable condition. If the honest answer to 'what would automatically prove a result wrong' is 'a human would just know', it is not a loop; an autonomous cycle would just grade its own output, which the skill puts off the verifier ladder as a negative gate.

Stripping the LLM leaves a fully deterministic job (codegen CLI + tsc + gate on exit code) with no model judgment changing the output at any step. The skill says deterministic work on a timer belongs in cron/Task Scheduler, not an agent loop; the rung-1 gate covers the deliverable, but no LLM judgment is needed to earn the loop label.

A time-triggered daily job with no iteration and no retry; a time trigger alone is not a reason to build an agent loop, and needing an LLM inside the job does not make it an autonomous loop. The only checkable condition is email delivery — a delivery guard, not a verifier of the subjective summary — so it's a scheduled one-shot draft-generator.

Recurs nightly; done is objectively checkable via the test suite exiting 0 (a rung-1 tool gate that can't be argued with); the agent acts end-to-end on the payments module. With a hard cap, budget stop, no-progress detection, and a scope fence, it can earn L3 unattended autonomy under the dial; the open question is prepaid vs metered capacity.

'Make it cleaner' is irreducibly subjective — no rung-1/2 gate verifies that a refactor is better; tests passing is a delivery guard on plumbing, not the deliverable. Running unattended overnight on metered billing with a subjective done-condition is spin-on-taste plus the overnight-runaway-bill failure mode; it cannot earn L3 without an objective gate on the valuable output.

Label choice and the first-response comment can't be objectively verified by any computational gate; correctness and quality are judgment calls, so the only real gate is a human. The output is also outward-facing (a public comment), which caps autonomy below L3 regardless of gate strength; this works as a draft-and-queue for human approval, not an autonomous loop.

'Best tagline' is a taste judgment — nothing can automatically distinguish good from bad marketing copy, so the verifier covers only plumbing, not the deliverable, which the skill calls a scheduled draft-generator. Publishing to the site is outward-facing and irreversible, capping autonomy at L2; a human must own the publish step.

The same model as maker and checker is off the verifier ladder — self-checking is a potentially negative gate that lets confidently-wrong code pass without external feedback. Running unattended on metered capacity without an independent rung-1 gate is the metered-burn and confident-wrong failure mode; L3 cannot be earned without a tool gate on the actual deliverable.

'Fixing whatever issues it finds' names no objective gate — Step 0 question 2 fails immediately. Without a gate the loop cannot converge (it spins on a subjective target or self-approves). L3 additionally requires a rung-1 tool gate, a scope must_not_touch fence, a budget cap, reversible output, and a proven manual pass — none present, so the validator errors on every axis of the autonomy ceiling.

The critical interview answers are unknown: cadence, gate, scope, and budget are all missing. Keep this at L0 and run discovery first: capture one bad result, design a gate that rejects it, define must_not_touch, and set a machine-readable budget cap before proposing any autonomous loop.
